lever: de-delegate to in-process de Pina (networkx _min_cycle_basis verbatim, all CPython-set-order parts kept in Python) with the O(m*n) Python Dijkstra 'lift' (94.5% of upstream time) replaced by ONE batched scipy csgraph.dijkstra over the lifted double-cover's sparse matrix; final shortest_path stays on nx for exact tie-break

parity.also_match_raw_nx: 57/58 (the 1 is a PRE-EXISTING non-unique-basis difference: fnx runs on a rebuilt component-ordered graph, not nx's SubgraphView)
parity.coverage: unweighted+weighted, multi-component, string nodes, trees, K4 distinct-weight (historical optimality fixture), adversarial two-triangles (PYTHONHASHSEED-sensitive)

headline: minimum_cycle_basis 7.6-10x faster (growing with n; ws120 8.1s->873ms), byte-exact to current fnx contract. Pure Python/scipy, no rebuild. Distances exact -> argmin + final path unchanged -> same basis incl. weighted.
note: old fnx delegated the whole algorithm to nx in-process, so nx timing == fnx-before
